element_introduction_order.mastery_context: Core mechanic enabled from beginning; protagonist can talk to demons instead of fighting
Purchased from scattered merchants; primary damage sources
Formed through dialogue with demons; requires alignment compatibility
Resource management mechanic; summoned demons consume magnetite while walking; running out damages demons
Nine possible alignment stances; affects demon behavior, recruitment eligibility, and area access; changes based on player story choices
Fuse 2-3 demons into single more powerful demon; only method to increase demon power (demons don't gain experience)
Scattered across world map; enable saving and fast travel between terminals in different Tokyo areas

element_introduction_order.element: Story Faction Areas (Order of Messiah Churches)
element_introduction_order.mastery_context: Alignment-gated areas; chaos-aligned players denied entry; represents ideological conflict escalation
After nuclear attack, transported 30 years forward; encounters ruins, demon-dominated landscape, escalating cult conflict

element_introduction_order.element: Competing Cults (Messiah Order vs Ring of Gaia)
element_introduction_order.mastery_context: Escalating ideological conflict determining endgame alignment; player choices become consequential

typical_relief_triggers: Reaching special terminals to save progress
Successful demon fusion creating more powerful ally
typical_tension_triggers: First-person dungeon navigation in unfamiliar areas
Magnetite depletion draining summoned demon health
Story escalation: demon invasion, nuclear attack, cult conflict
Alignment-incompatible demons refusing recruitment or cooperation
rhythm_pattern.pacing_descriptor: Gradual escalation from personal demon communication in near-future Tokyo to apocalyptic post-invasion landscape; tension compounds through alignment choices becoming irreversible and story stakes rising; relief provided by resource management and save terminals

spatial_grammar.optional_content_signal: Scattered merchants and special terminals across world map suggest exploratory optional content discovery; Cathedral locations serve as optional fusion hubs
spatial_grammar.secret_convention: Alignment restrictions gate certain areas and content; players may discover alternative paths based on Law/Neutral/Chaos and Light/Dark choices

difficulty_curve.early_game: Manageable; protagonist just acquiring demon communication abilities, learning demon recruitment through negotiation vs combat
difficulty_curve.mid_game: Escalating; alignment choices become permanent gates to certain areas and demons; resource management (magnetite) becomes critical; introduced to demon fusion for power progression
difficulty_curve.late_game: High stakes; post-apocalyptic setting introduces environmental hostility; competing cult factions with ideological opposition to player alignment; endgame paths determined by accumulated choices
notable_spikes: Nuclear attack story event transitioning to post-apocalyptic landscape
Introduction of alignment-gated cult areas restricting progression paths
Escalating conflict between Order of Messiah and Ring of Gaia presenting binary ideological choices

notable_level_patterns.description: First-person perspective dungeons alternating with overworld hub areas featuring merchants, save terminals, and faction buildings; Tokyo serves as persistent exploration space with scattered resource locations
Player choices lock off entire areas and NPC interactions based on Law/Neutral/Chaos alignment; creates branching level access and prevents certain demon recruitment paths
notable_level_patterns.example_level: Order of Messiah churches and Ring of Gaia strongholds (post-apocalypse)

notable_level_patterns.description: Magnetite depletion mechanic creates tension during extended dungeon crawls; terminals enable save checkpoints and restore resources, structuring dungeon depth
